McDonnell Douglas MD-11 takes its first flight.
July 25 George Carey, Bishop of Bath and Wells, is named as the new Archbishop of Canterbury in the Church of England.
Indian Airlines Flight 605, an Airbus A320-231 registered as VT-EPN, crashes shortly before landing killing 92 out of the 146 occupants on board.
Dissolution of the Soviet Union: Moldova declares independence from the Soviet Union, followed by Azerbaijan.
Harry Collinson, the planning officer for Derwentside District Council, is shot dead at Butsfield, County Durham, England, and the incident is caught on camera by a BBC regional news crew.
Ethiopian Civil War: The forces of the Ethiopian People's Revolutionary Democratic Front seize the capital Addis Ababa.
The South Korean presidential election is won by Kim Young-sam, the first non-military candidate elected since 1961.
June 20 Estonia adopts the kroon as currency, becoming the first former Soviet Republic to replace the Soviet rouble.
Violence breaks out between the African National Congress and the Inkatha Freedom Party in Boipatong, South Africa, leaving 45 dead.
The fifth summit of the Francophonie opens in Mauritius.
The first corrected images from the Hubble Space Telescope are taken.
June 5 The National Assembly of Venezuela designates Ramón José Velásquez as successor of suspended President Carlos Andrés Pérez.
Rwandan Patriotic Front troops capture Gisenyi, forcing the interim government into Zaire and ending the Rwandan genocide.
The Allied occupation of Berlin officially ends with a casing of the colors ceremony attended by U.S. president Bill Clinton.
Palau gains independence from the United Nations Trusteeship Council.

Iraq disarmament crisis: Iraqi scuba divers, under the direction of the United Nations Special Commission, dredge the Tigris near Baghdad. The divers find over 200 prohibited Russian-made missile instruments and components.
Eduard Shevardnadze, the Georgian head of state, survives an assassination attempt in Tbilisi.
The United States government stops funding the NSFNET, making the Internet a wholly privatized system.
____ United States presidential election: Incumbent Democratic President Bill Clinton defeats his Republican challenger, Bob Dole and Reform Party candidate Ross Perot.
A fire at La Planta prison in southwest Caracas, Venezuela, kills thirty prisoners.
Heavy rains kill more than 80 campers near Huesca, Spain.

June 26 Bertie Ahern is appointed as the 10th Taoiseach of the Republic of Ireland and Mary Harney is appointed as the 16th, and first female, Tánaiste, after their parties, Fianna Fáil and the Progressive Democrats respectively, win the ____ General Election.
March 13 India's Missionaries of Charity chooses Sister Nirmala, to succeed Mother Teresa as its leader.
About 50 people are killed in the Daïat Labguer (M'sila) massacre in Algeria.
Russia's government devalues the ruble, defaults on domestic debt, and declares a moratorium on repayment of foreign debt. This marks the beginning of ____ Russian financial crisis.
The High-Z Supernova Search Team becomes the first team to publish evidence that the universe is expanding at an accelerating rate.
Rudy Giuliani kicks off his "quality of life" campaign in New York City, focused on increasing civility and reducing nuisances like littering and reckless driving.
Former Warsaw Pact members Hungary, Poland, and the Czech Republic join NATO.

May 27 The International Criminal Tribunal for the former Yugoslavia in The Hague, Netherlands indicts Slobodan Milošević and four others for war crimes and crimes against humanity committed in Kosovo.
March 24 NATO launches air strikes against the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia, marking the first time NATO has attacked a sovereign state.
Approximate start of Autumn ____ Western Europe floods (particularly affecting the UK), precipitated by days of heavy rain.
Kenya Airways Flight 431 crashes off the Ivory Coast into the Atlantic Ocean, killing 169 people.

In Aden, Yemen, USS Cole is badly damaged by two Al-Qaeda suicide bombers, who place a small boat laden with explosives alongside the United States Navy destroyer, killing 17 crew members and wounding at least 39.
China joins the World Trade Organization.

January 16 Assassination of Laurent-Désiré Kabila: The President of the Democratic Republic of the Congo is shot in his office during the Second Congo War and rushed to Harare in Zimbabwe for medical treatment; his death will be announced two days later. He is succeeded by his son, Joseph Kabila.
August 10 ____ Angola train attack: 252 people are killed by UNITA in an attack on a train during the Angolan Civil War.
East Timor is admitted to the United Nations as the 191st member state; it also changes its official longform name from "Democratic Republic of East Timor" to "Democratic Republic of Timor-Leste".
Operation Enduring Freedom – Philippines: The Philippines and the United States begin a joint operation to combat Jihadist groups in the Philippines.
The WorldCom scandal breaks and the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission begins its investigation into WorldCom.
Former General François Bozizé seizes power through a military coup in the Central African Republic.
After acknowledging that it has produced enriched uranium, Iran agrees to suspend its nuclear program.
May 25 — A Boeing 727 aircraft is stolen from an airport in Luanda, Angola triggering an international terrorist investigation by the FBI and CIA. The aircraft and its pilots were never found.
An explosion in Ryanggang Province, North Korea, prompts speculation that North Korea is testing nuclear weapons.
January 4 NASA's Spirit rover lands on the surface of Mars.
European heads of state sign in Rome the Treaty and Final Act, establishing the first European Constitution.
November 22 Angela Merkel assumes office as the first female Chancellor of Germany.
Eris, the most massive known dwarf planet in the Solar System, is discovered by a team led by Michael E. Brown using images originally taken on October 21, 2003, at the Palomar Observatory.
YouTube, an American online video sharing and social media platform was founded by Steve Chen, Chad Hurley, and Jawed Karim headquartered in San Bruno, California.
South Korean Ban Ki-moon is elected as the new Secretary-General of the United Nations, succeeding Kofi Annan.
Stephen Harper is sworn in as the Prime Minister of Canada.
The roof of one of the buildings at the Katowice International Fair collapsed in Katowice, Poland, killing 65 and injuring 170.

The ____ Greensburg tornado moves through Kiowa County, Kansas, United States, striking the town of Greensburg and killing eleven residents. 95% of the town was destroyed, and the tornado was the first to be rated EF5 on the Enhanced Fujita scale.
The IPCC publishes its fourth assessment report, having concluded that global climate change is "very likely" to have a predominantly human cause.
The United Nations General Assembly adopts the Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples.
Cyclone Nargis passes through Myanmar, killing more than 138,000 people.
The Channel Island of Sark, a British Crown dependency, holds its first fully democratic elections under a new constitutional arrangement, becoming the last European territory to abolish feudalism.

____ financial crisis: Stocks fall sharply Monday on a triptych of Wall Street woe: Lehman Brothers' bankruptcy filing, Merrill Lynch's acquisition by Bank of America, and AIG's unprecedented request for short-term financing from the Federal Reserve.
Space Shuttle Atlantis is launched to refurbish the Hubble Space Telescope on May 11, landing at Edwards Air Force Base May 24.
The International Olympic Committee awards Rio de Janeiro the right to host the 2016 Summer Olympics.
Fire at the Lame Horse nightclub in Perm, Russia kills 156 people. The nightclub owner was sentenced to nearly ten years in prison.
year-old Paulette Gebara Farah disappears from her family's home located in Huixquilucan, State of Mexico.
Germany makes final reparation payment for World War I.
The Manila hostage crisis occurred near the Quirino Grandstand in Manila, Philippines killing 9 people, including the perpetrator, while injuring 9 others.
The ____ Rugby World Cup is held in and won by New Zealand who beat France in the final.
Occupy Wall Street protests begin in the United States. This develops into the Occupy movement which spreads to 82 countries by October.
The first nationwide Emergency Alert System test is conducted by the Federal Emergency Management Agency in the United States.
Air date of Kony ____ (viral documentary film) on YouTube.
The United Kingdom informs the World Health Organization about a novel coronavirus case originating from Saudi Arabia.
Sport Club Corinthians Paulista wins the Copa Libertadores by beating the Boca Juniors.
Saudi Arabia rejects a seat on the United Nations Security Council, making it the first country to reject a seat on the Security Council. Jordan takes the seat on December 6.

The Emergency Alert Systems of five different television stations across the U.S. states of Montana, Michigan, Wisconsin, and New Mexico are hijacked, airing a false "Zombie apocalypse" hoax. It is amongst one of the largest cybersecurity breaches in EAS history.
A magnitude 7.2 earthquake struck Bohol, Philippines, leaving 222 dead, 8 missing, and 976 people injured.
Australia wins the ____ Hockey World Cup.
The Japanese space agency, JAXA, launches uncrewed spaceprobe Hayabusa2 from the Tanegashima Space Center on a six-year round-trip mission to Ryugu to collect rock samples.
Washington DC metro opens Phase 1 of the silver line to Tysons Corner, Reston, and Loudoun county.
The ____ Cricket World Cup is held in Australia and New Zealand with Australia defeating New Zealand in the final.
A global climate change pact is agreed at the COP 21 summit in Paris, committing all countries to reduce carbon emissions for the first time.
SpaceX lands an uncrewed Falcon 9 rocket, the first reusable rocket to successfully enter orbital space and return.
The government of India announces the demonetisation of certain banknotes, causing prolonged cash shortages in the weeks that follow and significant disruption throughout the economy.
The Helsinki District Court gives Jari Aarnio, the former head of anti-drugs police, to ten-year prison sentence for drug crimes as part of a rare police corruption scandal in Finland.
NASA's Juno spacecraft enters orbit around Jupiter and begins a 20-month survey of the planet.
The Qatar diplomatic crisis of ____–18 starts, as Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, the United Arab Emirates and other Arab countries block Qatari access to their seas and air.
Fortnite Battle Royale releases, becoming one of the biggest video games in the world and a cultural phenomenon.
____ Barcelona attacks: 22-year-old Younes Abouyaaqoub drives a van into pedestrians on La Rambla in Barcelona, killing 13 people and injuring at least 130 others.

The IPCC releases its Special Report on Global Warming of 1.5 °C, warning that "rapid, far-reaching and unprecedented changes in all aspects of society" are needed to ensure that global warming is kept below 1.5 °C.
The world's last male northern white rhinoceros dies in Kenya, making the subspecies functionally extinct.
January 28 ____ Finnish presidential election: Incumbent president Sauli Niinistö is reelected on the first round of voting.
____ Busy Bee crash: A plane crashes shortly after takeoff in a densely populated area of Goma, Democratic Republic of the Congo, killing all 19 people on board and at least 10 more on the ground.

The European Parliament votes by 348 to 278 in favour of the EU Directive on Copyright in the Digital Single Market, which expands legal liability for websites and includes the controversial Article 13.
November 9 The Supreme Court of India awards a holy site in Ayodhya, Uttar Pradesh to Hindus, rejecting a Muslim claim. The move is likely to spur sectarian disputes.
(52768) 1998 OR2, a near-Earth asteroid that is 2 kilometers (1.2 mi) wide, makes a close approach of 0.042 AU (6.3 million km; 16 LD) to Earth. It will not approach closer than this until 2079.
13 Hong Kong pro-democracy lawmakers resign en masse, in response to four lawmakers' disqualification made by the government.
____ Moldovan presidential election: Former Prime Minister and Minister of Education Maia Sandu is elected as the 6th President of Moldova, becoming the first woman to ever hold the post.
Tigray War: The Tigray Defense Force seizes the Tigrayan capital Mekelle shortly after the Ethiopian government declares a ceasefire.
The Lucy spacecraft is launched by NASA, the first mission to explore the Trojan asteroids.
____ Guinean coup d'état: Guinea's President Alpha Condé is detained by an elite military unit led by a former French legionnaire, Lt. Col. Mamady Doumbouya, claiming to have seized power.
The Atlantic hurricane Fiona hits Guadeloupe, Puerto Rico, the Dominican Republic, the Lucayan Archipelago, Bermuda, and Eastern Canada.
____ monkeypox outbreak: An outbreak of monkeypox begins when the first monkeypox virus case is reported in London, the United Kingdom.
The World Health Organization (WHO) declares the recent monkeypox outbreak a Public Health Emergency of International Concern, as the number of reported cases exceeds 17,000 in 75 countries.
Ukrainian war: The Nova Kakhovka dam in the Russian-controlled region of Kherson is destroyed, threatening the region with devastating floodwaters.

____ Argentine presidential election: Following the first round on 22 October ____, Javier Milei wins in the second round of the election, assuming office on 10 December with Victoria Villarruel as his vice president.
In the Netherlands, the governing coalition collapses and Prime Minister Mark Rutte announces his upcoming resignation.
The Central Committee of the Communist Party of Vietnam elects Tô Lâm as the new General Secretary of the Communist Party of Vietnam, succeeding Nguyễn Phú Trọng, who died on July 19.
A powerful earthquake with a magnitude of 7.4 strikes off the eastern coast of Taiwan, with small tsunamis reaching heights of 20–30 cm (8–12 in) hitting Okinawa Prefecture, Japan.
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announces that a ceasefire deal has been agreed to end fighting with Hezbollah in Lebanon.
A multi-vehicle collision involving a bus carrying migrants deported from Iran, a motorcycle and a truck kills at least 79 people in Herat Province, Afghanistan.
The ____ Vanuatuan general election is held.
September 21 The UK, Canada, and Australia announce their formal recognition of Palestine as a sovereign state, with France also doing so the next day.
The 56th World Economic Forum is held in Davos, Switzerland.
____ Portuguese presidential election: Socialist candidate António José Seguro is elected President of Portugal in the second round, beating Chega leader André Ventura.
First Secretary of the Communist Party of Cuba Miguel Díaz-Canel confirms that Cuba is in talks with the Trump administration to find solutions to the oil blockade in the country.
